title: Esports DB
description: PandaScore esports data — matches, teams, players, tournaments, leagues.

game_overview.title: Game Coverage Overview
game_overview.description: How many leagues, series and tournaments does each game have? A quick snapshot of coverage across all titles.
game_overview.sql: SELECT v.name AS game, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM leagues WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS leagues, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM series WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS series, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM tournaments WHERE videogame_id = v.id) AS tournaments FROM videogames v ORDER BY leagues DESC

leagues_by_game.title: Leagues for a Game
leagues_by_game.description: Browse every league for a specific title. Try: Counter-Strike, Valorant, Dota 2, LoL, Overwatch, Rocket League, PUBG.
leagues_by_game.sql: SELECT v.name AS game, l.name AS league, l.url, COUNT(DISTINCT s.id) AS series_count FROM leagues l JOIN videogames v ON v.id = l.videogame_id LEFT JOIN series s ON s.league_id = l.id WHERE v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%' GROUP BY l.id ORDER BY series_count DESC

coverage_by_year.title: Historical Coverage by Year
coverage_by_year.description: How many tournaments were recorded per game per year? All filters are optional. Leave blank to see everything.
coverage_by_year.sql: SELECT strftime('%Y', t.begin_at) AS year, v.name AS game, COUNT(*) AS tournaments FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id WHERE t.begin_at IS NOT NULL AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') AND (:year = '' OR strftime('%Y', t.begin_at) = :year) GROUP BY year, v.id ORDER BY year DESC, tournaments DESC

major_events.title: Major Events (S-Tier)
major_events.description: The most prestigious events in esports — Majors, Worlds, Grand Finals. Leave Game blank for all titles.
major_events.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at, CASE t.winner_type WHEN 'Team' THEN (SELECT name FROM teams WHERE id = t.winner_id) ELSE NULL END AS winner FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.tier = 's' AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') ORDER BY t.begin_at DESC LIMIT 100

ongoing_tournaments.title: Ongoing Tournaments
ongoing_tournaments.description: Tournaments currently in progress. Leave Game blank for all titles. Try: Counter-Strike, Valorant, Dota 2.
ongoing_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.begin_at <= datetime('now') AND (t.end_at IS NULL OR t.end_at >= datetime('now')) AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') ORDER BY t.tier, t.begin_at ASC LIMIT 50

upcoming_tournaments.title: Upcoming Tournaments
upcoming_tournaments.description: Tournaments not yet started, sorted by earliest start date. Leave Game or Tier blank to skip that filter. Game: Counter-Strike, Valorant… Tier: s, a, b, c, d.
upcoming_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.begin_at > datetime('now') AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') AND (:tier = '' OR t.tier = :tier) ORDER BY t.begin_at ASC LIMIT 100

completed_tournaments.title: Completed Tournaments
completed_tournaments.description: Recently finished tournaments with results. Leave Game or Tier blank to skip that filter. Game: Counter-Strike, Valorant… Tier: s, a, b, c, d.
completed_tournaments.sql: SELECT t.name, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.tier, t.prizepool, t.begin_at, t.end_at, CASE t.winner_type WHEN 'Team' THEN (SELECT name FROM teams WHERE id = t.winner_id) ELSE NULL END AS winner FROM tournaments t JOIN videogames v ON v.id = t.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = t.league_id WHERE t.end_at < datetime('now') AND t.end_at IS NOT NULL AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') AND (:tier = '' OR t.tier = :tier) ORDER BY t.end_at DESC LIMIT 50

live_matches.title: Live Matches
live_matches.description: Matches happening right now. Leave Game blank for all titles. Try: Counter-Strike, Valorant, Dota 2.
live_matches.sql: SELECT m.begin_at, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.name AS tournament, t.tier, m.match_type, m.number_of_games, COALESCE(tm1.name, p1.name) AS team_1, COALESCE(tm2.name, p2.name) AS team_2, m.live_embed_url FROM matches m JOIN videogames v ON v.id = m.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = m.league_id JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = m.tournament_id LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo1 ON mo1.match_id = m.id AND mo1.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm1 ON mo1.opponent_id = tm1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p1 ON mo1.opponent_id = p1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Player' LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo2 ON mo2.match_id = m.id AND mo2.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id AND x.id > mo1.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm2 ON mo2.opponent_id = tm2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p2 ON mo2.opponent_id = p2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Player' WHERE m.status = 'running' AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') ORDER BY m.begin_at ASC

upcoming_matches.title: Upcoming Matches
upcoming_matches.description: Scheduled matches not yet started, sorted by earliest kick-off. Leave Game or Tier blank to skip that filter. Game: Counter-Strike, Valorant… Tier: s, a, b, c, d.
upcoming_matches.sql: SELECT m.scheduled_at, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.name AS tournament, t.tier, m.match_type, m.number_of_games, COALESCE(tm1.name, p1.name) AS team_1, COALESCE(tm2.name, p2.name) AS team_2 FROM matches m JOIN videogames v ON v.id = m.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = m.league_id JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = m.tournament_id LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo1 ON mo1.match_id = m.id AND mo1.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm1 ON mo1.opponent_id = tm1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p1 ON mo1.opponent_id = p1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Player' LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo2 ON mo2.match_id = m.id AND mo2.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id AND x.id > mo1.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm2 ON mo2.opponent_id = tm2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p2 ON mo2.opponent_id = p2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Player' WHERE m.status = 'not_started' AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') AND (:tier = '' OR t.tier = :tier) ORDER BY m.scheduled_at ASC LIMIT 100

completed_matches.title: Completed Matches
completed_matches.description: Recent match results with scores. Leave Game or Tournament blank to skip that filter. Game: Counter-Strike, Valorant… Tournament: partial name, e.g. IEM Katowice.
completed_matches.sql: SELECT m.end_at, v.name AS game, l.name AS league, t.name AS tournament, t.tier, m.match_type, COALESCE(tm1.name, p1.name) AS team_1, mo1.score AS score_1, COALESCE(tm2.name, p2.name) AS team_2, mo2.score AS score_2 FROM matches m JOIN videogames v ON v.id = m.videogame_id JOIN leagues l ON l.id = m.league_id JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = m.tournament_id LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo1 ON mo1.match_id = m.id AND mo1.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm1 ON mo1.opponent_id = tm1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p1 ON mo1.opponent_id = p1.id AND mo1.opponent_type = 'Player' LEFT JOIN match_opponents mo2 ON mo2.match_id = m.id AND mo2.id = (SELECT MIN(x.id) FROM match_opponents x WHERE x.match_id = m.id AND x.id > mo1.id) LEFT JOIN teams tm2 ON mo2.opponent_id = tm2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p2 ON mo2.opponent_id = p2.id AND mo2.opponent_type = 'Player' WHERE m.status = 'finished' AND (:game = '' OR v.name LIKE '%' || :game || '%') AND (:tournament = '' OR t.name LIKE '%' || :tournament || '%') ORDER BY m.end_at DESC LIMIT 100

team_roster.title: Team Roster
team_roster.description: Active players on a team. Enter a full or partial team name. Try: Team Liquid, Natus Vincere, Cloud9.
team_roster.sql: SELECT te.name AS team, p.name AS player, p.first_name, p.last_name, p.role, p.nationality, p.active FROM players p JOIN teams te ON te.id = p.current_team_id WHERE te.name LIKE '%' || :team || '%' ORDER BY p.role, p.name

team_schedule.title: Team Schedule & Results
team_schedule.description: Recent and upcoming matches for a team — all statuses in one view, newest first. Enter a full or partial team name. Try: Team Liquid, Natus Vincere, Cloud9.
team_schedule.sql: SELECT m.scheduled_at, m.status, v.name AS game, t.name AS tournament, t.tier, m.match_type, MAX(CASE WHEN mo_rn.rn = 1 THEN COALESCE(tm.name, p.name) END) AS team_1, MAX(CASE WHEN mo_rn.rn = 1 THEN mo_rn.score END) AS score_1, MAX(CASE WHEN mo_rn.rn = 2 THEN COALESCE(tm.name, p.name) END) AS team_2, MAX(CASE WHEN mo_rn.rn = 2 THEN mo_rn.score END) AS score_2 FROM matches m JOIN videogames v ON v.id = m.videogame_id JOIN tournaments t ON t.id = m.tournament_id JOIN match_opponents mof ON mof.match_id = m.id AND mof.opponent_type = 'Team' JOIN teams ft ON ft.id = mof.opponent_id AND ft.name LIKE '%' || :team || '%' LEFT JOIN (SELECT match_id, opponent_id, opponent_type, score, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY match_id ORDER BY id) AS rn FROM match_opponents) mo_rn ON mo_rn.match_id = m.id LEFT JOIN teams tm ON mo_rn.opponent_id = tm.id AND mo_rn.opponent_type = 'Team' LEFT JOIN players p ON mo_rn.opponent_id = p.id AND mo_rn.opponent_type = 'Player' GROUP BY m.id ORDER BY m.scheduled_at DESC LIMIT 100

videogames.description: Games covered in the database (Counter-Strike, Valorant, Dota 2, etc.)

series.description: Competitive seasons (series) within leagues, grouped by year

tournaments.description: Individual tournaments within series, with tier and prize pool information

players.description: Professional players with nationality, role, and active status

match_opponents.description: Teams or players participating in a match, with scores and win/loss result
